name: Eurylaimidae
description: Typical broadbills are brightly colored, stout-billed passerines found in tropical Asia and Africa. Their distinctive wide, flat bills give the family its common name.

children.name: Eurylaimus
children.description: Eurylaimus is a genus of broadbills found in Southeast Asia, known for their vivid plumage and wide bills.

children.name: Eurylaimus javanicus
children.description: The banded broadbill is a striking bird with a pinkish breast, yellow-banded black wings, and a bright blue bill. It inhabits lowland forests across Southeast Asia.

children.name: Cymbirhynchus
children.description: Cymbirhynchus is a monotypic genus of broadbill with a distinctive black, white, and maroon plumage pattern.

children.name: Cymbirhynchus macrorhynchos
children.description: The black-and-red broadbill is a strikingly patterned bird found in mangroves and wetlands of Southeast Asia. Its deep maroon underparts and black upperparts make it unmistakable.

children.name: Serilophus
children.description: Serilophus is a genus of broadbill with a distinctive silvery breast and bold wing pattern.

children.name: Serilophus lunatus
children.description: The silver-breasted broadbill has a unique pale grey breast set against rufous and blue plumage. It inhabits montane and lowland forests from the Himalayas to Southeast Asia.

children.name: Psarisomus
children.description: Psarisomus is a monotypic genus of broadbill with an exceptionally long tail relative to its body size.

children.name: Psarisomus dalhousiae
children.description: The long-tailed broadbill is a vibrant green bird with a blue cap and a long graduated tail. It is highly social and often moves in noisy flocks through forest canopies.

children.name: Corydon
children.description: Corydon is a monotypic genus of broadbill characterized by its dark, sooty plumage and thick pale bill.

children.name: Corydon sumatranus
children.description: The dusky broadbill is a dark, brownish-black bird with a distinctive pale pinkish or horn-colored bill. It inhabits lowland and hill forests across mainland Southeast Asia and Sumatra.
